name: ICoordinationStrategy
description: Contract for multi-agent coordination strategy implementations. Orchestrates multiple agent adapters to collaboratively complete a coordination task. Supports patterns like supervisor delegation, agent handoff, and parallel dispatch.

methods.name: coordinate

params.name: agents
params.description: Registry of named agent adapters available for coordination

params.name: task
params.description: The coordination task to execute, including prompt, subtasks, context_data, and timeout

params.name: context
params.description: Current workflow execution context with variables, deps, and metadata

methods.description: Coordinate multiple agents to complete a task. The strategy determines how agents interact (supervisor delegates, handoff chains, parallel fan-out). Returns a CoordinationResult with the synthesized output and per-agent execution details.

behavioral_expectations: Must implement structural subtyping (Protocol) — no explicit inheritance required
The coordinate() method is async — agent interactions involve I/O
CoordinationTask contains: prompt (str), subtasks (list[str]), context_data (dict), timeout (float | None)
CoordinationResult contains: output (str), agent_results (dict[str, AgentResult]), strategy_name (str), metadata (dict)
Must handle agent failures gracefully — a single agent failure should not crash the entire coordination
Must respect timeout constraint to prevent infinite coordination loops
Three built-in patterns: supervisor (one agent delegates to others), handoff (sequential agent chain), parallel_dispatch (concurrent fan-out/fan-in)
Agent selection from the registry is strategy-dependent — not all agents need to participate
Must propagate execution context to all participating agents
